architecture.name: IndustrialSecure IoT/OT Platform
architecture.description: Production-grade industrial IoT/OT platform implementing the Purdue Model with edge-to-cloud telemetry, real-time anomaly detection, SCADA integration, secure firmware updates, and air-gap-safe OT network segmentation across Levels 0–5

components.name: Field Devices (Sensors/Actuators)
components.technology: Modbus RTU / HART 7, 4-20mA, discrete I/O
components.description: Physical sensors (temperature, pressure, flow, vibration) and actuators on the plant floor

components.name: PLC Data Collector (Modbus TCP/IP)
components.technology: Modbus TCP/IP RFC 793, libmodbus 3.1.8, C99
components.description: Polls PLCs via Modbus TCP, normalises register maps, exposes structured telemetry to Level-2

components.name: Safety Instrumented System (SIS)
components.technology: IEC 61511, SIL-2 rated, Modbus TCP/IP read-only feed
components.description: Independent SIS providing emergency shutdown. Read-only integration to DCS; no write-path from IT network

components.name: OPC-UA Gateway (Kepware)
components.technology: Kepware KEPServerEX 6.14, OPC-UA 1.05, x509 certificates
components.description: Aggregates Modbus/Ethernet-IP/PROFINET device data into unified OPC-UA server. Signed sessions, certificate-based authentication

components.name: DCS / SCADA HMI
components.technology: Wonderware SCADA 2023, Windows Server 2022 (hardened, CIS L2)
components.description: Operator HMI providing real-time process visualisation and control interlocks. Air-gapped from corporate LAN

components.name: SCADA Interface Layer (C++/Python bridge)
components.technology: C++17 OPC-UA SDK (open62541 v1.3.8) + Python 3.12 wrapper, Protobuf 3
components.description: Bidirectional bridge converting OPC-UA typed data to Protobuf messages for northbound Kafka ingestion. Read-only path to field network

components.name: Historian Service (On-Prem, SCADA data)
components.technology: OSIsoft PI System 2023 (PI Server 3.4.440), PI AF 2.10
components.description: Long-term process historian storing compressed time-series at 1s resolution, 10-year retention

components.name: Edge Gateway (ARM64, Ubuntu 22.04)
components.technology: NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX 16GB, Ubuntu 22.04 LTS, K3s v1.29.2, dm-verity
components.description: ARM64 edge node aggregating OPC-UA and MQTT streams, running local ML inference and forwarding to cloud via WireGuard tunnel

components.name: MQTT Broker (Eclipse Mosquitto 2.0)
components.technology: Eclipse Mosquitto 2.0.18, MQTT 5.0, TLS 1.3, mutual-mTLS
components.description: High-throughput MQTT broker for device telemetry. ACL-based topic isolation per device group. QoS 1 for sensor data, QoS 2 for commands

components.name: Edge ML Inference (TensorFlow Lite)
components.technology: TensorFlow Lite 2.16, ONNX Runtime 1.17, Python 3.12, CUDA optional
components.description: On-device anomaly detection models (LSTM autoencoder, isolation forest) running at 100ms inference cycle. Triggers local alerts without cloud round-trip

components.name: WireGuard VPN (Edge-to-Cloud Tunnel)
components.technology: WireGuard 1.0.0 (kernel module), ChaCha20-Poly1305, Curve25519 key exchange
components.description: Cryptographic tunnel from edge site to AWS VPC. Pre-shared key rotation every 30 days. Split-tunnel routing: only IoT traffic routed to cloud

components.name: Industrial DMZ Firewall
components.technology: Palo Alto PA-3440 (PAN-OS 11.1), Purdue DMZ, Zone-based policy
components.description: Hardware next-gen firewall enforcing the Level-3.5 DMZ. ICS protocol deep-packet inspection for Modbus, OPC-UA, DNP3. Default-deny between OT and IT

components.name: HAProxy (Load Balancer)
components.technology: HAProxy 2.9.4 LTS, PROXY Protocol v2, TLS 1.3 termination
components.description: Layer-7 load balancer distributing ingestion and API traffic across RKE2 node pools. Health checks every 2s, passive circuit-breaking

components.name: Apache Kafka Cluster (3 brokers)
components.technology: Apache Kafka 3.7.0 (KRaft mode, no ZooKeeper), 3-broker cluster, TLS + SASL/SCRAM
components.description: Durable event streaming backbone. Topics: raw-telemetry (72h retention), alerts (7d), commands (24h), audit-log (90d). Replication factor 3

components.name: Kafka Connect (MQTT Connector)
components.technology: Kafka Connect 3.7.0, kafka-connect-mqtt 1.6.0, Schema Registry (Avro 1.11)
components.description: Bridges MQTT broker topics to Kafka topics with Avro schema enforcement. Dead-letter topic for malformed messages

components.name: Time-Series Ingestion Service
components.technology: Go 1.22.1, gRPC 1.62, Protocol Buffers 3, Prometheus metrics
components.description: High-throughput Kafka consumer writing normalised telemetry to InfluxDB at 50k pts/s. Batches writes with 100ms flush interval. Duplicate detection via device+timestamp dedup key

components.name: Alert Processing Service
components.technology: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.110, Celery 5.3.6, Redis 7.2 (task queue)
components.description: Stateful CEP engine consuming Kafka alert stream. Applies threshold, rate-of-change, and ML-derived rules. Routes severity-3+ alerts to PagerDuty and SCADA HMI

components.name: Device Management Service
components.technology: Rust 1.75.0 (tokio async runtime), Axum 0.7.4, SQLx 0.7.4
components.description: Device registry and lifecycle manager. Tracks device certificates, last-seen timestamps, firmware versions, and expected-vs-actual configuration hashes

components.name: Firmware Update Service
components.technology: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.110, libsodium (Ed25519 signing), SWUpdate 2023.11
components.description: Secure OTA firmware distribution with mandatory Ed25519 signature verification. Staged rollout: canary (1%) → pilot (10%) → full fleet. Rollback on validation failure

components.name: Certificate Authority (step-ca)
components.technology: Smallstep step-ca 0.25.2, ACME v2, EST (RFC 7030), PKCS#11 HSM backend
components.description: Internal CA issuing x509 certificates for device mTLS, MQTT clients, OPC-UA sessions, and service mesh. 90-day device certs, 8h service certs

components.name: Vault (HashiCorp, Secrets Management)
components.technology: HashiCorp Vault 1.15.6, PKCS#11 auto-unseal (AWS KMS), AppRole + Kubernetes auth
components.description: Centralised secrets store for DB credentials, API keys, PKI roots. Dynamic secrets for InfluxDB and TimescaleDB with 1h TTL leases

components.name: Consul (Service Discovery)
components.technology: HashiCorp Consul 1.18.0, mTLS mesh, ACL tokens, gossip encryption
components.description: Service registry and health-check engine for all on-prem microservices. Provides DNS-based discovery and connect sidecar proxies for zero-trust east-west traffic

components.name: RKE2 Kubernetes Cluster (On-Prem)
components.technology: RKE2 v1.29.2+rke2r1, CIS Benchmark hardened, FIPS 140-2 mode, Cilium CNI 1.15
components.description: Air-gap-capable Kubernetes distribution running all Level-4 microservices. Cilium enforces network policy at kernel level. OPA Gatekeeper for admission control

components.name: InfluxDB (Time-Series Data)
components.technology: InfluxDB OSS 2.7.5, Flux query language, Snappy compression
components.description: Primary time-series store for raw device telemetry. Retention policies: hot (30d NVMe), warm (180d SSD), cold (MinIO offload). 100M pts/day write capacity

components.name: TimescaleDB (Aggregated Metrics)
components.technology: TimescaleDB 2.14.2 (PostgreSQL 16.2 extension), continuous aggregates, columnar compression
components.description: Stores 1-minute and 1-hour downsampled aggregates. Powers Grafana dashboards and ML training datasets. Partitioned by site_id + device_type

components.name: MinIO (Object Storage, S3-compat)
components.technology: MinIO RELEASE.2024-02-26, AES-256-SSE, erasure coding (8+4), WORM mode
components.description: On-prem S3-compatible object store for firmware images, ML model artifacts, InfluxDB cold backups, and compliance audit archives. WORM locks on audit buckets

components.name: Grafana Dashboards (Monitoring UI)
components.technology: Grafana Enterprise 10.3.3, LDAP/SAML SSO, RBAC, datasource proxying
components.description: Operational dashboards for plant engineers and security analysts. Datasources: InfluxDB (live telemetry), TimescaleDB (aggregates), Loki (logs), Tempo (traces). MFA enforced

components.name: Loki + Tempo (Log/Trace Stack)
components.technology: Grafana Loki 2.9.6, Grafana Tempo 2.3.1, OpenTelemetry Collector 0.95.0
components.description: Centralised structured log aggregation and distributed tracing for all Level-4 services. Logs shipped to SIEM via Loki → Vector → Splunk

components.name: OT Network Intrusion Detection (Claroty)
components.technology: Claroty xDome 4.7 (passive OT IDS), Modbus/DNP3/OPC-UA protocol parsers
components.description: Passive network tap on the OT LAN. Detects protocol anomalies, unexpected device connections, and known ICS CVE exploitation patterns. Zero-touch deployment

components.name: SIEM (Splunk Enterprise Security)
components.technology: Splunk Enterprise Security 7.3.0, ES Content Update, MITRE ATT&CK for ICS
components.description: Correlates OT IDS alerts, Kafka audit events, firewall logs, and Linux auditd into ICS-specific detection rules. NERC-CIP compliance reports generated monthly

components.name: AWS IoT Core (Cloud Telemetry)
components.technology: AWS IoT Core (MQTT 5.0 over TLS 1.3), IoT Rules Engine, Fleet Indexing
components.description: Cloud MQTT endpoint receiving forwarded edge telemetry via WireGuard tunnel. Device shadows for last-known state. IoT Rules route to Timestream and Lambda

components.name: AWS Timestream (Cloud Time-Series)
components.technology: Amazon Timestream for LiveAnalytics, magnetic tier (1yr), memory tier (7d)
components.description: Serverless cloud time-series database for cross-site analytics and ML training data lake. Queried via Grafana Cloud datasource and SageMaker feature engineering jobs

components.name: AWS SageMaker (Anomaly Model Training)
components.technology: Amazon SageMaker Studio 2024.01, PyTorch 2.2, scikit-learn 1.4, MLflow 2.10
components.description: Cloud ML training pipeline retraining anomaly detection models weekly from Timestream data. Signed model artifacts pushed to MinIO via S3 sync for edge deployment

components.name: AWS S3 (Firmware + Model Repository)
components.technology: Amazon S3 (SSE-KMS, Object Lock WORM, versioning enabled)
components.description: Cloud-side source-of-truth for signed firmware images and trained ML models. Object Lock prevents tampering. Firmware Updater Service syncs from here to on-prem MinIO

components.name: AWS CloudWatch + Security Hub
components.technology: AWS CloudWatch Logs Insights, AWS Security Hub (NIST CSF standard), GuardDuty IoT
components.description: Cloud-side observability. GuardDuty IoT detects device-level anomalies. Security Hub aggregates findings and maps to NIST CSF 2.0 controls for executive reporting

connections.description: Field sensors and actuators polled by PLC data collector over plant-floor wiring
Safety-critical I/O hardwired to SIS; no software path to IT network
PLC collector exposes register data to OPC-UA Gateway via isolated OT LAN
SIS provides read-only SIL status to SCADA HMI; no write-back path
Kepware OPC-UA server supplies live process values to DCS/SCADA HMI
SCADA Interface Layer subscribes to OPC-UA MonitoredItems for northbound bridging
SCADA HMI writes process data to on-prem Historian via OPC-UA Historical Access
SCADA Interface Layer publishes normalised telemetry to MQTT Broker on site/plant/device topics
Historian PI Web API exposes historical data to Time-Series Ingestion Service on demand
Edge Gateway subscribes to device telemetry topics from MQTT Broker for local processing
Edge Gateway forwards windowed feature vectors to Edge ML Inference service
Edge Gateway initiates WireGuard tunnel to AWS VPC for cloud-bound telemetry
Kafka Connect MQTT source connector bridges broker topics into Kafka raw-telemetry topic
Edge ML Inference publishes local anomaly alerts to MQTT alerts/ topic
WireGuard tunnel terminates at AWS VPC; edge publishes to AWS IoT Core endpoint
Industrial DMZ firewall permits WireGuard tunnel from edge to AWS only; blocks all other outbound
Firewall SPAN port feeds raw OT traffic to Claroty IDS for passive inspection
HAProxy load-balances Kafka producer connections across 3 brokers
HAProxy routes gRPC ingestion calls to Time-Series Ingestion Service pods
Time-Series Ingestion Service consumes raw-telemetry Kafka topic
Alert Processing Service consumes alerts Kafka topic and raw-telemetry for CEP
Kafka Connect produces Avro-encoded records to raw-telemetry and commands topics
Time-Series Ingestion Service batch-writes telemetry points to InfluxDB
Alert Processing Service writes alert records and device correlation data to TimescaleDB
Alert Processing Service forwards severity-3+ alerts to Splunk SIEM via HTTP Event Collector
Device Management Service stores device registry, cert expiry, and firmware version in TimescaleDB
Device Management Service fetches dynamic DB credentials from Vault
Firmware Update Service reads signed firmware images from MinIO S3-compat API
Firmware Update Service publishes OTA update commands to device command topics
Firmware Update Service requests firmware signing certificates from step-ca

connections.protocol: HTTPS / Vault PKI secrets engine / port 8200
connections.description: step-ca uses Vault as upstream PKI root for intermediate CA signing
RKE2 pods authenticate to Vault via projected service account tokens (Kubernetes auth method)
Consul agents on each RKE2 node maintain cluster membership and service health-check registry
RKE2 cluster hosts InfluxDB as StatefulSet with dedicated NVMe storage class
RKE2 cluster hosts TimescaleDB as StatefulSet with persistent volume claims
InfluxDB cold-tier offload job writes Parquet snapshots to MinIO for long-term archival
Grafana queries TimescaleDB for aggregate metric dashboards
Grafana queries InfluxDB for real-time telemetry panels

connections.protocol: Loki HTTP / port 3100, Tempo gRPC / port 9095
connections.description: Grafana datasource connections to Loki (logs) and Tempo (traces) for unified observability
Claroty xDome forwards OT IDS alerts and inventory events to Splunk ES

connections.protocol: AWS IoT Rules → Timestream WriteRecords API
connections.description: IoT Core Rules Engine routes device telemetry directly into Timestream via built-in action
IoT Core pushes connection logs and message delivery metrics to CloudWatch

connections.protocol: AWS SDK / HTTPS / Timestream Query API
connections.description: SageMaker training jobs pull historical telemetry from Timestream as training dataset
Trained and quantised model artifacts pushed to S3 firmware+model repository with Ed25519 signature
Automated sync job replicates signed models and firmware from AWS S3 to on-prem MinIO nightly

connections.protocol: Security Hub Findings API → Splunk ES HEC / port 8088
connections.description: Security Hub forwards cloud-side GuardDuty IoT findings to on-prem Splunk for unified SOC view

trust_boundaries.name: Level-0: Physical Process Layer
controls: Physical access control (badge + biometric)
Hardwired I/O only (no IP connectivity at Level-0)
SIL-2 rated safety loops per IEC 61511
Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) procedures for maintenance
Cable tamper detection on critical field bus runs

trust_boundaries.name: Level-1: Basic Control Layer (PLC / SIS)
controls: Air-gapped PLC subnet (no Layer-3 routing to IT)
Modbus TCP restricted to authorised HMI source IPs via PLC ACL
Read-only OPC-UA subscription from SIS (no write registers exposed)
USB ports physically disabled on all PLC workstations
Industrial unidirectional gateway (data diode) for SIS feed

trust_boundaries.name: Level-2: Supervisory / SCADA Layer
controls: Dedicated SCADA VLAN, no default gateway to corporate LAN
OPC-UA mutual certificate authentication (SHA-256, 2048-bit RSA minimum)
Whitelist-only firewall rules: SCADA → Historian unidirectional
Windows Server 2022 CIS Level-2 hardening on HMI hosts
No internet access from SCADA subnet; patching via WSUS over isolated VLAN
Annual IEC 62443-3-3 gap assessment for Zone 2

trust_boundaries.name: Level-3.5: Industrial DMZ
controls: Palo Alto NGFW with ICS deep-packet inspection (Modbus, OPC-UA, DNP3, EtherNet/IP)
Default-deny policy: all traffic explicitly allowed by service, port, direction
Claroty passive IDS on SPAN port — zero OT network impact
WireGuard is ONLY outbound tunnel permitted to cloud (UDP 51820)
No inbound connections from Level-4 to Level-3 or lower
Jump host with PAM (CyberArk) required for any admin access to Level-3
Network flow logging to Splunk SIEM with 1-year retention (NERC-CIP)
trust_boundaries.iec62443_zones: DMZ — Conduit between OT and IT

trust_boundaries.name: Level-4: Enterprise / Site Operations Network
controls: RKE2 Kubernetes CIS-hardened, FIPS 140-2 kernel mode
OPA Gatekeeper admission control: no privileged containers, no hostNetwork, required labels
Vault dynamic secrets: DB credentials rotated every 1 hour
mTLS for all service-to-service communication via Consul Connect
Cilium network policy: default-deny namespace isolation
WireGuard tunnel to AWS VPC (only path to Level-5)
RBAC via LDAP integration: operator / engineer / admin roles
Splunk SIEM correlation with MITRE ATT&CK for ICS detection rules

trust_boundaries.name: Level-5: Cloud / Enterprise WAN
controls: AWS IoT Core device policies: each device certificate restricted to its own topic namespace
VPC private endpoints for Timestream and S3 (no public internet traversal)
AWS KMS CMK for all data at rest in S3 and Timestream
IAM least-privilege roles per service (SageMaker cannot write IoT Core, etc.)
CloudTrail API logging with 7-year retention in immutable S3 bucket
GuardDuty IoT Threat Detection enabled on all IoT Core endpoints
S3 Object Lock WORM on firmware and model repositories
AWS Security Hub mapped to NIST CSF 2.0 controls
trust_boundaries.nerc_cip: CIP-011 (data handling in cloud per NERC-CIP cloud guidance)

trust_boundaries.name: OT Safety Boundary (SIS Isolation)
controls: Physical separation: SIS on dedicated copper LAN, no shared switches with DCS
Read-only data diode for SIS status feed to SCADA (Waterfall Security unidirectional gateway)
No remote access path to SIS from any network (maintenance via local console only)
IEC 61511 Functional Safety Management system with independent SIL verification
SIS firmware changes require two-person integrity rule and offline signing
Annual SIS proof-test documented in compliance historian
trust_boundaries.nerc_cip: CIP-007 (port and service management on safety systems)

data_flows.name: Real-Time Sensor Telemetry (Field → Cloud)
data_flows.description: End-to-end path for live sensor data from field devices to cloud time-series

data_flows.name: Cloud Telemetry Sync (Edge → AWS IoT Core)
data_flows.description: Forwarding of aggregated device telemetry to cloud via WireGuard tunnel
data_flows.frequency: 10 Hz per device (100 devices per site)
security_controls: AWS KMS encryption at rest in Timestream

data_flows.name: OTA Firmware Update (Cloud → Edge Device)
data_flows.description: Secure firmware delivery from cloud repository to field device via staged rollout
data_flows.frequency: As needed (average 2x per month per device fleet)

data_flows.name: ML Model Lifecycle (Cloud Train → Edge Deploy)
data_flows.description: Weekly retrained anomaly models flow from SageMaker to on-prem MinIO to edge devices
data_flows.frequency: Weekly retraining; model deployment within 24h of training completion
security_controls: MinIO versioning + checksum verification on edge pull

data_flows.name: Security Alert Escalation (OT IDS → SOC)
data_flows.description: OT anomaly events detected by Claroty flow to Splunk for SOC analyst triage
security_controls: PagerDuty escalation for CIP-008 incident response

data_flows.name: Historical Process Data (Historian → Analytics)
data_flows.description: On-demand queries from Grafana and ingestion service to PI Historian for trend analysis
data_flows.frequency: On-demand dashboard queries; daily batch export for analytics
